## Daily summary

Themes: AI Operations / Agent Control • Model + API Changes • Tools Worth Testing • Data Infrastructure / Verification / Scraping.

Tonight's brief tracks AI Operations / Agent Control, Model + API Changes, Tools Worth Testing, and Data Infrastructure / Verification / Scraping. Synthesized Nightly Librarian run with 19 promoted item(s), 40 scored item(s), and 21 rejected item(s). The lead source signal is Show HN: peerd — AI agent harness that runs entirely in the browser: peerd is an open-source (Apache 2.0) in-browser AI agent harness that leverages the browser sandbox and p2p WebRTC for agent isolation and A2A without external middlemen. The operator read is Open-source agent substrate that directly addresses prompt-injection isolation — relevant to MCP/agent builders. Supporting context: Cloudflare: Self-Managed OAuth now available to all developers (Platform capability that lets solo builders own auth without extra vendors); GLM-5.2 is a step change for open agents (Best open agentic model right now — directly relevant to anyone building agents on open weights). Monitor-only context stays out of the publish list until reviewed: Vercel Workflow SDK 5 beta now compresses run/step payloads (zstd) (Real cost/storage reduction for agent-history persistence on Workflow SDK); [AINews] It's Meta-Harness Summer (Trend signal for agent-harness builders).

## Worth mentioning

### 1. Show HN: peerd — AI agent harness that runs entirely in the browser

- Source: [github.com](https://github.com/NotASithLord/peerd)
- Category: AI Operations / Agent Control
- Published: 2026-06-26T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: builder_reported, publish_public
- Product relevance: none

Source facts:
- peerd is an open-source (Apache 2.0) in-browser AI agent harness that leverages the browser sandbox and p2p WebRTC for agent isolation and A2A without external middlemen.

Builder/operator takeaway: Open-source agent substrate that directly addresses prompt-injection isolation — relevant to MCP/agent builders.

Uncertainty: Early v0.x preview; not yet in Chrome/AMO stores and the wasm-Linux layer depends on a non-open-source engine.

### 5. Show HN: Pure Effect — reproduce production bugs locally without a DB

- Source: [pure-effect.org](https://pure-effect.org)
- Category: Tools Worth Testing
- Published: 2026-06-26T00:00:00.000Z
- Labels: builder_reported, publish_private
- Product relevance: none

Builder/operator takeaway: Practical testability/debugging pattern for TS solo devs with low adoption cost.

Uncertainty: v0.8.0, single-author production use; long-term maintenance and ecosystem are unproven.
